title: Mahindra XUV.e8 (Electric XUV700): Expected Price, Range & 2026 Launch
description: Mahindra's flagship electric SUV, the XUV.e8, is set for a much-anticipated launch in mid-2026. Discover the battery specifications, all-wheel drive system, and cutting-edge interior design.
content: Mahindra is poised to lead the premium electric vehicle market with the XUV.e8, the all-electric iteration of the hugely popular XUV700.

Having been spotted testing across India, the XUV.e8 maintains the bold profile of its internal combustion engine (ICE) counterpart, featuring a sleek closed grille, a full-width LED light strip, and aerodynamic alloy wheels.

Inside, the cabin is a haven for technology enthusiasts, featuring an impressive triple-screen arrangement that spans the dashboard, incorporating AI-enhanced advanced driver-assistance systems (ADAS) and state-of-the-art connectivity tech.

Built on Mahindra's innovative INGLO skateboard platform, the vehicle is expected to come equipped with a substantial 80 kWh battery, available in both Rear-Wheel Drive (RWD) and high-performance All-Wheel Drive (AWD) variants.
With aggressive pricing aimed at undercutting luxury electric vehicles, the XUV.e8 is set to compete directly with the Tata Harrier EV and the BYD Atto 3 when it arrives in showrooms later this year.

specs.engine: Dual Motor AWD / Single Motor RWD

pros: Impressive 80 kWh battery for worry-free long-distance driving
Generous 3-row seating arrangement within an electric platform
Proven structural safety of the XUV700 coupled with INGLO EV technology
cons: Higher starting price expected compared to the ICE XUV700
Substantial kerb weight due to the large battery system

title: Toyota Urban Cruiser Ebella: Launch Date, Expected Price & Features
description: Toyota is gearing up to unveil the Urban Cruiser Ebella, its new electric SUV, in India on April 15, 2026. Discover details about its battery specifications, range, and pricing.
content: Toyota is making a significant entry into the Indian electric vehicle market with the much-awaited Urban Cruiser Ebella, set to debut on April 15, 2026.
Constructed on a specialized EV skateboard platform, the Ebella aims to blend Toyota's renowned reliability with cutting-edge electric mobility technology.
It’s anticipated to come equipped with a strong 60 kWh battery pack, offering a practical driving range of over 450 km on a single charge.
The interior will feature a modern cabin with a sizable touchscreen infotainment system, sophisticated connected car technology, and Level 2 ADAS for improved safety.
With an expected pricing range of ₹18 Lakh to ₹23 Lakh, the Ebella will compete head-to-head with the Maruti Suzuki eVitara, Tata Curvv EV, and MG ZS EV.
pros: Reputable reliability and build quality from Toyota
Roomy interior thanks to the dedicated EV skateboard platform
Standard Level 2 ADAS available in mid and top variants
cons: Higher anticipated starting price compared to ICE rivals
Development of public fast-charging infrastructure in Tier 2/3 cities is still in progress

title: Tata Sierra EV 2026: Bookings Open, Specs, and Range Details
description: The iconic Tata Sierra makes its comeback in an all-electric form. Discover the battery specifications, anticipated pricing, and interior highlights of the 2026 Sierra EV.
content: The moment we've all been waiting for has arrived! Tata Motors has officially launched bookings for the eagerly awaited Sierra EV, breathing new life into a legendary model for the electric age.
Remaining remarkably faithful to its heritage, the 2026 Sierra EV showcases the signature alpine windows, a spacious lounge-inspired interior, and a sleek, modern front design.
Built on Tata's cutting-edge Gen 2 Acti.ev platform, it guarantees ample cabin space, featuring a panoramic sunroof and standard Level 2 Advanced Driver Assistance Systems (ADAS).
The Sierra EV is projected to be available with two battery pack options, targeting a realistic range that should alleviate any concerns about running out of charge on long drives.
Situated just above the Harrier EV, the Sierra is set to tap into the premium lifestyle SUV market, going head-to-head with the forthcoming Mahindra XEV 9e.
pros: Iconic retro-modern design featuring alpine windows
Spacious, lounge-like seating in the rear
Advanced Level 2 ADAS and V2L (Vehicle to Load) technology
Impressive ground clearance suited for Indian roads
cons: Premium pricing may put it beyond the reach of budget-conscious buyers
Heavy kerb weight might impact driving dynamics

title: Hyundai Boulder Concept 2026: Features, Design & Is It a Ford Bronco Rival?
description: Explore the Hyundai Boulder Concept, a rugged off-road SUV with a ladder-frame structure, featuring impressive 37-inch tires, 'Art of Steel' design, and cutting-edge trail technology.
content: The Hyundai Boulder Concept makes a bold statement in the extreme off-road vehicle market, representing a significant departure from Hyundai's typical crossover style.
Constructed on a sturdy ladder-frame chassis, this 'Art of Steel' SUV boasts enormous 37-inch mud-terrain tires and remarkable ground clearance, ready for the toughest trails.
Inside, it harmonizes rugged practicality with high-tech features, including tactile physical knobs, fold-out tray tables, and an advanced real-time off-road navigation system.
The exterior is eye-catching with a striking Liquid Titanium finish, safari-style roof windows, coach doors, and a functional double-hinged tailgate.
Though it remains a concept for the time being, the Boulder platform hints at an eagerly awaited midsize pickup truck that is expected to begin production by 2030.
pros: Innovative double-hinged tailgate and coach doors
Striking, boxy design with impressive 37-inch tires
cons: Mainly focused on the US market

title: Hyundai Verna 2026 Gets New Features Update
description: Hyundai Verna 2026 updated with new features, improved comfort, and better driving experience in the sedan segment.
content: With the new updates, the Hyundai Verna 2026 remains a strong contender in the sedan segment.
The latest version offers improved features, greater comfort, and improved driving dynamics.
It remains a top choice for customers seeking a premium sedan experience in India.

title: Renault Duster 2026: Legendary SUV is Back in India
description: Renault Duster 2026: The legendary Renault Duster is back in India. This time, it has a bold new design, hybrid technology, and strong SUV credentials, making it one of the most trending cars to make a comeback in India.
content: Renault Duster 2026: Renault has launched a new version of one of India's most iconic SUVs.
The Renault Duster 2026 has a completely new exterior, modern features, and enhanced safety features.
The Renault Duster 2026 is a strong contender in the mid-size SUV segment.
pros: Modern SUV design with rugged DNA
cons: Has to compete with the likes of Creta and Seltos

title: Hyundai Creta 2026: Most Trending Family SUV
description: Hyundai Creta remains one of India's top-trending SUVs in 2026 thanks to premium features, comfort, and strong city-highway balance.
content: Creta continues to trend among buyers looking for a complete family SUV package.
It offers a feature-rich cabin, refined drive quality, and dependable day-to-day usability.
For city + highway mixed use, it stays one of the most searched options in India.

title: Kia Seltos 2026: Tech-Focused Trending SUV
description: Kia Seltos is trending for its design, connected features, and strong road presence in the midsize SUV segment.
content: Seltos attracts buyers who prioritize style and technology in daily driving.
Its cabin quality and infotainment setup remain key reasons for strong popularity.
For buyers wanting a modern SUV feel, Seltos continues to be a top shortlist car.

title: Tata Nexon 2026: Safety-Led Trending Compact SUV
description: Tata Nexon remains a trending compact SUV pick due to strong safety reputation, bold styling, and practical ownership value.
content: Nexon stays highly popular among safety-conscious buyers in India.
Its road presence, features, and balanced real-world usability keep demand strong.
For buyers comparing under-15-lakh SUVs, Nexon remains one of the most followed options.
cons: Rear seat space could be better
